meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Using Git and AI for Student Developers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to debug faster, build your portfolio, and ship your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial and error; it's about systematic isolation. By integrating Git with AI, you create a versioned history of failures that allows Claude Code to analyze your diffs, identify the root cause, and suggest precise fixes. Stop guessing and start using your commit history as a diagnostic tool.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red text, three hours into a project that worked perfectly yesterday. The silence of your editor is deafening, and the frustration of being stuck is the single biggest barrier to your career as a developer. This isn't just a bug; it's a rite of passage every senior engineer has endured.

But here is the secret: the best builders don't solve problems alone. They leverage the synergy between version control and AI agents to turn debugging into a structured, repeatable process. By mastering the Git-AI workflow, you stop fighting your code and start orchestrating it, transforming your biggest coding hurdles into your most valuable learning milestones.

steps.title: Establish a Clean Git Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, ensure your current state is captured. A clean Git status allows AI agents to see exactly what changed since your last working commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current working directory against the last commit. Identify which specific file changes triggered the recent runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: git status && claude --reason 'Identify breaking changes since last commit'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure with Diffs
steps.description: Use Git to extract the exact code changes that introduced the bug. Providing the diff to an AI reduces hallucinations and increases fix accuracy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the diff of the last three commits. Identify the logic error in the function causing the stack trace.
steps.claude_code_command: git diff HEAD~3 | claude --stdin 'Explain why this diff causes the current error'

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Run an AI agent against your codebase to cross-reference your recent changes with the error logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am getting a null pointer exception. Scan the project structure and suggest where the data flow is being interrupted.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Analyze the project for null pointer exceptions in the current branch'

steps.title: Test-Driven AI Patching
steps.description: Don't just fix the code; write a test case to prevent regression. Use AI to generate the unit test before applying the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that reproduces the bug I just identified in the previous step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --write-test 'Create a reproduction test case for the identified bug'

steps.title: Commit and Verify
steps.description: Once the test passes, commit the fix with a descriptive message. This builds a robust portfolio history that shows employers you know how to debug professionally.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this fix into a professional conventional commit message.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'fix: resolve runtime error in data processing pipeline'

pro_tips: Always commit before asking an AI to refactor; it gives you a one-click 'undo' button.
Use 'git bisect' to find the exact commit that introduced a bug before feeding it to Claude Code.
Treat your error logs as data; pipe them into a text file and pass that file to the AI for higher accuracy.
Ask your AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix, not just the code; this is how you actually learn.
Keep your branch names descriptive (e.g., 'fix/auth-token-refresh') to provide context to your AI tools.
content.next_steps: Now that you have fixed your first bug using the Git-AI workflow, apply this to a new feature branch. Practice creating a 'broken' branch, fixing it, and merging it back into your main branch to solidify your Git mastery.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to overcome any error code. Stop waiting for permission to be a professional—start building the software that defines your career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Git to extract the exact code changes that introduced the bug. Providing the diff to an AI reduces hallucinations and increases fix accuracy.
Run an AI agent against your codebase to cross-reference your recent changes with the error logs.
Don't just fix the code; write a test case to prevent regression. Use AI to generate the unit test before applying the fix.
Once the test passes, commit the fix with a descriptive message. This builds a robust portfolio history that shows employers you know how to debug professionally.
